label: 3 Tier Architecture
description: An architecture seen in the last presentation.

label: View of Data
description: The focus of this presentation.

label: Multiple Views
description: Views existing at the presentation level; reasons for needing them are revealed in this presentation.

label: Data Abstraction
description: Means hiding the complexity; needed to understand views of data.

label: Database Goal
description: To store and retrieve data conveniently and effectively.

label: Data Storage
description: Involved for storing and retrieving data.

label: Storage Data Structures
description: May involve simple or complex data structures for data storage.

label: Complexity
description: Information to be hidden by data abstraction.

label: Essential Details
description: Provided when hiding complexity.

label: Abstract Data
description: A short and sweet answer; desired over complete data.

label: Complete Data
description: Not desired; involves encountered complexities.

label: Levels of Data Abstraction
description: Several levels exist, specifically 3 levels.

label: Physical Level
description: The lowest level of abstraction; the database level; deals with how data are stored.

label: Logical Level
description: Deals with what data are actually stored and the relationship among data stored.

label: View Level
description: The topmost level; highest level of abstraction; deals with users and their access.

label: Low Level Data Structures
description: Used at the physical level, especially for multimedia data.

label: What Data Stored
description: Describes the data actually to be stored in the databases.

label: Data Relationships
description: Describes the relationship among the data stored in the database.

label: Simple Data Structures
description: Used at the logical level.

label: Physical Data Independence
description: Exists so that if any changes are made in the logical level, it will not affect the physical level.

label: Application Level
description: Where the logical level is, and applications reside.

label: Schema
description: The structure of the database.

label: Database Administrator
description: Involved in all levels; writes all the logic, especially at the logical level.

label: User Access
description: Granting privileges to users, managed at the view level.

label: User-System Interaction
description: Done at the view level through interfaces.

label: Interfaces
description: Web, mobile, GUI; used for user interaction with the system.

label: Security of Multiple Views
description: An important aspect to focus on at the view level.
